Load a causaldata dataset in RCausalML-ready form

Source: R/causaldata_integration.R
load_causaldata.Rd

Loads a dataset from the causaldata package and returns covariates X, treatment w, outcome y, and optionally instrument Z, plus the full data frame and citation information. Use these with SLearner, DRLearner, causal_forest, OrthoIVLearner, etc. Always cite the causaldata package and the original data source when publishing or reporting results.

Usage

load_causaldata(name, subset_abortion = TRUE)

Arguments

name

Character. One of nsw_mixtape, cps_mixtape, abortion, close_college, social_insure, black_politicians, thornton_hiv, nhefs_complete. See list_causaldata_datasets.

subset_abortion

If name == "abortion", subset to 15–19 year-old gonorrhea (bf15 == 1) and drop NA in key variables. Default TRUE.

Value

A list with components:

X

Model matrix of covariates (numeric).

w

Treatment vector (0/1 or numeric for close_college).

y

Outcome vector.

Z

Instrument (only for close_college).

data

Original data.frame from causaldata.

citation

List with package, data_source, textbook for use in reports; run citation("causaldata") for BibTeX.

Details

This helper standardizes selected textbook datasets into a common shape used throughout RCausalML: X (covariates), w (treatment), y (outcome), and optionally Z (instrument for IV examples).

References

Huntington-Klein, N., & Barrett, M. (2024). causaldata: Example data sets for causal inference textbooks. R package. https://github.com/NickCH-K/causaldata.

Cunningham, S. (2021). Causal Inference: The Mixtape. Yale University Press. https://mixtape.scunning.com.

Huntington-Klein, N. (2021). The Effect: An Introduction to Research Design and Causality. https://theeffectbook.net.

Hernan, M. A., & Robins, J. M. (2020). Causal Inference: What If. Chapman & Hall/CRC.

See also

list_causaldata_datasets, citation("causaldata")

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(RCausalML)
# List supported datasets
list_causaldata_datasets()

# NSW experimental data (Mixtape)
d <- load_causaldata("nsw_mixtape")
sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger")
sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y)
estimate_ate(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y)
# Cite the data
citation("causaldata")
print(d$citation)

# IV: college proximity and wages (Card)
d_iv <- load_causaldata("close_college")
# Use d_iv$X, d_iv$w (educ), d_iv$y (lwage), d_iv$Z (nearc4) with OrthoIVLearner
} # }
